intro:
Dating app popularity varies significantly by country. Tinder dominates globally but faces strong regional competitors: Happn and Fruitz in France, Badoo in Southern Europe and Latin America, Bumble in English-speaking markets, Grindr for gay men worldwide.

Embir is open worldwide on the web, while early community density grows city by city where dating apps leave the biggest gaps.

comparisonTable.embir: Primary launch market — founding community active
Free for all users in all markets

sections.title: Why Embir is open worldwide
sections.content: These three markets share a common pattern: high dating app adoption, dissatisfaction with existing options (particularly around LGBTQ+ inclusivity and profile authenticity), and users willing to try a verified, inclusive alternative. The founding community in all three markets is actively shaping the platform's early culture.
highlights: Worldwide web access with city-by-city community density
Founding community in all three markets is active
French interface for France, English for UK and USA

verdict: Embir is available worldwide on the web, with verified profiles, bidirectional filtering, universal LGBTQ+ inclusivity, and free access at launch. Early density grows city by city instead of closing the product to a few countries.
ctaText: Join Embir in your country — core connection features are free

faq.q: Which countries is Embir available in?
faq.a: Embir is available worldwide on the web. Community growth is managed city by city so local density can build without closing access to founding members elsewhere.

faq.q: What language is Embir available in?
faq.a: Embir is available in French and English. Additional languages will be added as expansion continues.
France is one of Embir's three primary launch markets. The founding community in Paris and major French cities is active and growing.
